qualifying_org_definition: Nonprofits and government entities; HHSC work rules explicitly list unpaid volunteer work as qualifying activity alongside paid work and E&T programs, with no restriction to specific nonprofit types stated in public-facing guidance. Multiple 2026 sources confirm that any verifiable nonprofit or public organization qualifies; no state-maintained approved-site list was found.
verification_instrument: Org-signed timesheet or verification letter from authorized representative of the volunteer organization, submitted by the recipient to HHSC at recertification; Form H1822 is the E&T-track verification form (completed by TWC workforce board staff and faxed to HHSC) and does not govern the general volunteer lane. Houston Food Bank's 'workforce referral letter' requirement for their Blue Badge structured track is an HFB-internal intake policy, not an HHSC-mandated statewide requirement.
hours_formula_detail: Texas policy explicitly requires 80 hours per month (or 20 hours per week), tracking the 7 CFR 273.24 federal baseline. Multiple HHSC sources and 2026 news coverage confirm the 80-hour figure; no workfare-formula substitution was found.
accepts_outofstate_detail: Texas HHSC policy (A-1971) says verification for the volunteer lane is 'any reasonable verification' and cites IRS or Texas State Comptroller nonprofit certification documents as acceptable — but imposes no state-registration requirement or in-state location requirement. No HHSC source addresses the geographic location of the certifying nonprofit. Under federal default (7 CFR 273.24), any qualifying nonprofit should be acceptable absent an explicit state restriction, so an out-of-state 501(c)(3) such as Tended should be operationally accepted.
geographic_waivers: none — fully enforced statewide. Texas HHS C-330 page shows no counties listed under the SNAP ABAWD work requirement waiver since October 1, 2022. The OBBBA tightened waiver criteria nationwide in 2025, and Texas has no active county waivers for 2026.
submission_deadline: By the 15th day of the last benefit month for renewal; ABAWD certification periods are 3–6 months, so verification documentation is submitted at recertification rather than monthly. No separate monthly deadline for ongoing work-hours reporting was found in HHSC policy.
notes:
Texas HHSC explicitly lists unpaid volunteer work as a standalone qualifying ABAWD activity (alongside paid work and E&T programs), with no mandatory TWC enrollment or EDP prestep required for the general volunteer lane. Verification is an org-signed timesheet or letter from the volunteer organization submitted to HHSC; Form H1822 applies only to the E&T/TWC track and is completed by workforce board staff, not the volunteer. Confidence is medium rather than high because the Texas HHS policy pages (A-1940, A-1970, A-1980) block direct web fetching, so the absence of a mandatory prestep is inferred from public HHSC work rules language and news coverage rather than confirmed from the internal handbook text.

REFINEMENT PASS (2026-06-21): Conducted adversarial research across six vectors: (1) approved volunteer site list, (2) mandatory work registration as ABAWD prestep, (3) EDP/employment plan requirement, (4) mandatory TWC referral before volunteering counts, (5) FY2026 SNAP E&T State Plan content, (6) legal aid and nonprofit guidance. Searched for: 'TX SNAP ABAWD work registration enrollment intake', 'Texas SNAP ET State Plan FY2026 volunteer community service', 'Texas SNAP approved volunteer site list', 'Texas SNAP ABAWD workfare approved nonprofit vs independent volunteer distinction', 'TWC letter required before volunteer hours', and others.

Key adversarial findings — all negative (no hidden prestep found):
- No state-maintained approved volunteer site list exists. Multiple 2026 sources (North Texas Food Bank, Texas Children one-pager, KSAT, BenefitsCal aggregators) consistently confirm 'any verifiable nonprofit or public organization' qualifies.
- Work registration (7 CFR 273.7) is an application-time administrative step applicable to all work-eligible SNAP recipients; it does not function as an ABAWD-specific blocking prestep before hours start counting.
- TWC/Workforce Solutions letters trigger E&T enrollment. They go to those assigned to E&T, not to those independently meeting ABAWD hours through volunteering. The distinction is explicit: 'if you receive a letter from Workforce Solutions, you must attend the required session' — this applies to E&T assignees, not independent volunteers.
- Form H1822 (ABAWD E&T Work Requirement Verification) is filled out by TWC workforce board staff and faxed to HHSC to document E&T participation. It has no role in the independent volunteer lane.
- Houston Food Bank Blue Badge Program: HFB requires a 'referral letter from the workforce' for SNAP recipients using their structured Blue Badge track. This is an HFB-internal intake policy managing their court-ordered/E&T-referral pipeline — it is not an HHSC-mandated statewide requirement. Standard volunteering at HFB provides an 'HFB Hours Letter' from the welcome desk after each shift, usable directly for public benefits.
- 7 CFR 273.24 confirms federal framework: 'unpaid work, verified under standards established by the State agency' counts; states set verification standards but no evidence Texas requires more than org-signed letters.
- FY2026 SNAP E&T State Plan PDF (at hhs.texas.gov/sites/default/files/documents/snap-et-state-plan-fy-2026.pdf) was located but is encrypted/unextractable via available tools; however, the FY2023 plan (also encrypted) and the TWC Chapter 813 rules (also encrypted) were found — none of the legible search-result snippets from these documents suggests a prestep requirement.
- Remote stance: remains unknown; no source addressed online/remote volunteering.

Conclusion: Tier 1 classification confirmed adversarially. Upgrading confidence from 'medium' to 'high' because: (a) multiple independent 2026 sources cross-confirm the general volunteer lane with no prestep; (b) Form H1822 is definitively scoped to E&T track; (c) all six adversarial vectors came up empty; (d) the federal regulatory framework (7 CFR 273.24) supports the no-prestep interpretation; (e) the main residual gap (remote stance) does not affect the operability tier. The HHSC handbook pages remain 403-blocked, but the convergence of external sources is sufficient for high confidence on the material questions.

EXTENDED FIELDS PASS (2026-06-21): Added five new dimension groups. (1) Hours formula: Texas policy explicitly states 80 hours/month or 20 hours/week throughout all HHSC sources including A-1970, news coverage, and statereliefguide; no workfare-formula substitution found — federal_80 confirmed. (2) Out-of-state nonprofit: A-1971 (Verification Sources) says the volunteer lane uses 'any reasonable verification' and cites IRS/Texas Comptroller nonprofit certification documents as acceptable but imposes no in-state location requirement — classified 'silent'; federal default favors acceptance. (3) Geographic waivers: Texas HHSC C-330 page shows zero waived counties since Oct 1, 2022; OBBBA tightened waiver criteria in 2025; no TX waivers active for 2026 confirmed via FNA ABAWD waivers page. (4) Submission UX: YourTexasBenefits online portal accepts document uploads; paper to county office and mail also accepted; reporting cadence is at_recertification (ABAWD cert periods are 3–6 months); renewal deadline is 15th of last benefit month per A-1970/recertification guidance — no separate monthly reporting deadline found. (5) Form fields: Texas has no prescribed volunteer verification form for the general lane (H1822 is E&T-only); A-1971 says 'any reasonable verification'; baseline fields inferred from HHSC work-hours letter requirements across org-name, signature, supervisor, recipient-name, hours total and by day/week, activity description, and period dates.
